description: All water on, under, and above Earth's surface — including oceans, lakes, rivers, groundwater, ice caps, and water vapour in the atmosphere.

contents.name: totalVolumeKm3
contents.description: Estimated total volume of water in Earth's hydrosphere in cubic kilometres.

contents.name: saltWaterPercentage
contents.description: Percentage of the hydrosphere that is salt water (approximately 97.5%).

contents.name: freshWaterPercentage
contents.description: Percentage of the hydrosphere that is fresh water (approximately 2.5%).

contents.name: globalSeaLevelMm
contents.description: Real-time or periodically updated global mean sea level relative to a baseline in millimetres.

contents.name: isPartOf
contents.description: The Hydrosphere is part of Earth.

contents.name: interactsWith
contents.description: The Hydrosphere interacts with the Biosphere through the water cycle, aquatic habitats, and nutrient transport.

description: A large body of saltwater that covers approximately 71% of Earth's surface. The five oceans are the Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Southern, and Arctic.

contents.name: name
contents.description: Name of this ocean (Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Southern, Arctic).

contents.name: areaMillion_km2
contents.description: Surface area of this ocean in millions of square kilometres.

contents.name: averageDepthM
contents.description: Average depth of this ocean in metres.

contents.name: surfaceTemperatureCelsius
contents.description: Real-time sea surface temperature telemetry in degrees Celsius.

description: A body of still fresh or saline water surrounded by land and not directly connected to the ocean. Lakes occupy approximately 1.8% of Earth's land surface.

contents.name: name
contents.description: Name of this lake.

contents.name: area_km2
contents.description: Surface area of this lake in square kilometres.

contents.name: isFreshwater
contents.description: Indicates whether this is a freshwater (true) or saline (false) lake.

description: A natural flowing watercourse that moves towards an ocean, sea, lake, or another river. Rivers are primary conduits of the freshwater cycle.

contents.name: name
contents.description: Name of this river.

contents.name: lengthKm
contents.description: Total length of this river in kilometres.

contents.name: flowRateM3s
contents.description: Current volumetric flow rate at the river mouth in cubic metres per second.

description: A persistent mass of ice that forms where the accumulation of snow exceeds its ablation over many years. Glaciers store approximately 69% of Earth's fresh water.

contents.name: name
contents.description: Name of this glacier.

contents.name: area_km2
contents.description: Surface area of this glacier in square kilometres.

contents.name: massBalanceMmWaterEquiv
contents.description: Annual mass balance of this glacier in millimetres of water equivalent. Negative values indicate net ice loss.

contents.name: retreatRateMperYear
contents.description: Current rate of glacial retreat in metres per year.
